name: No-Bake Pineapple Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup grape nuts cereal (1/2 cup for a 10-inch plate)
1 tablespoon hazelnut, walnut or canola oil
1 teaspoon unsalted butter, melted (2 tbsp for a 10-inch plate)
1/2 large egg white (1 tbsp for a 10-inch plate)
2 tablespoons fruit juice or water, or as needed (1 tbsp for a 10-inch plate)
1 can crushed pineapple (unsweetened or in light syrup) (20 oz)
3/4 cup low-fat cream cheese, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Crumble graham crackers into a bowl and process until crumbs form.
3. Add cereal, sugar, oil, butter, egg white, and juice or water. Pulse until evenly moistened.
4. Test the moisture by pressing a spoonful of crumbs with your finger. If needed, add more juice or water and pulse once or twice.
5. Press the mixture into an 8-inch springform pan or 10-inch pie plate, forming an even layer around the sides.
6. Spread remaining crumbs evenly over the bottom. Top with wax paper and press to form an even layer, avoiding excess in the corners.
7. Bake for 7 minutes, then cool completely on a wire rack before filling.
8. Drain yogurt for at least 20 minutes using a fine-mesh strainer. Discard whey.
9. Drain crushed pineapple juice and transfer to a bowl. Press lightly with spoon to release juice.
10. Place cottage cheese in a strainer over a bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and press down firmly.
11. Transfer pressed cottage cheese into a blender or food processor with cream cheese. Blend until smooth, stopping to scrape sides as needed.
12. Add sugar, vanilla, pineapple extract, and drained yogurt. Pulse until well blended.
13. Scrape sides and pulse several times.
14. Add reserved 1/2 cup crushed pineapple juice and lemon juice to a saucepan. Sprinkle gelatin over the mixture and let sit for 3 minutes.
15. Stir the mixture over low heat just until gelatin is dissolved; do not boil.
16. With blender or food processor running, add the gelatin mixture and pulse to blend.
17. Pour filling into crust and refrigerate at least 3 hours or overnight before serving. Cover with plastic wrap once firm.
